meta.title: Middle East Causal Network - Directed Graph of Conflict Dynamics
meta.methodology: Directed acyclic graph (with feedback loops) mapping causal relationships between structural causes, proximate triggers, accelerants, actors, resources, and outcomes

edge_types.enables: Creates necessary conditions for the target without directly causing it
edge_types.triggers: Directly initiates or activates the target
edge_types.amplifies: Intensifies or escalates an existing dynamic
edge_types.constrains: Limits, reduces, or inhibits the target
edge_types.correlates: Statistically associated without clear causal direction

edges.evidence: Sykes-Picot borders created multi-sectarian states where colonial powers exploited communal divisions (Dodge, 2014)
Artificial borders undermined organic state formation and national cohesion (Anderson, 2006)
Kurdish homeland was partitioned across four states by post-WWI treaties (McDowall, 2004)
Baseline water scarcity compounds climate-driven precipitation decline (World Bank MENA Water Report, 2023)
Syrian drought 2006-2010 displaced 1.5 million, contributing to uprising conditions (Kelley et al., PNAS 2015)
Youth unemployment above 25% was a primary driver of 2011 protest mobilization (Campante & Chor, 2012)
Unemployed youth overrepresented in online radicalization pipelines (Mercy Corps, 2015)
Youth demographics provide recruitment pool for sectarian militias (ICG, 2016)
Sunni-Shia divide maps onto Iran-Saudi competition for regional influence (Nasr, 2006)
Sectarian identity provides organizing logic for proxy recruitment (Salloukh et al., 2015)
Pre-existing fault lines lower threshold for sectarian mobilization cycles (Haddad, 2011)
Rentier state theory: oil revenue substitutes for taxation, reducing accountability (Ross, 2012)
Hydrocarbon dependency defines economic structure of Gulf states (Hertog, 2010)
Institutional fragility determined which Arab Spring states experienced state collapse (Brownlee et al., 2015)
Weak state capacity allows war economies to supplant formal governance (SIPRI, 2020)
Libya, Yemen, Syria fragmented along pre-existing fault lines when institutions failed (ICG, 2021)
Unresolved Palestinian grievances are the root context for the October 7 attack and response (UN OCHA, 2024)
Abraham Accords bypassed Palestinian issue, both reflecting and deepening marginalization (Khalidi, 2020)
Palestinian cause is the most mobilizing content category in MENA social media (Al Jazeera Media Institute, 2024)
Turkish military operations explicitly target Kurdish autonomous governance (ICG Turkey/Syria reports)
Iran-Saudi competition is the primary driver of proxy warfare across the region (Mabon, 2019)
Saudi lobbying contributed to US JCPOA withdrawal decision (Parsi, 2021)
Beijing rapprochement partially constrains direct bilateral escalation (MEI, 2023)
Colonial governance models prioritized security over civilian institution-building (Owen, 2004)
Mandate-era security apparatuses became templates for post-independence authoritarianism (Sluglett, 2014)
Iranian enrichment capability was the central issue driving JCPOA and its collapse (IISS, 2020)
Nuclear threshold status raises stakes of Iran-Israel confrontation (Einhorn, Brookings, 2024)
Saudi MBS stated Kingdom will match Iranian nuclear capability (CBS interview, 2018)
Democratic deficit was the primary grievance driving 2011 Arab uprisings (Bellin, 2012)
Authoritarian systems misallocate resources, worsening youth unemployment (World Bank, 2019)
Syrian civil war, an Arab Spring consequence, created conditions for eventual regime collapse
Post-Arab Spring state collapse in Syria and Iraq created the space for ISIS emergence (Lister, 2015)
Civil wars triggered by Arab Spring produced the largest displacement crisis since WWII (UNHCR, 2020)
JCPOA collapse removed diplomatic guardrails, enabling Iran-Israel escalation spiral (IISS, 2024)
Maximum pressure campaign context facilitated Soleimani strike decision (Samore, 2020)
Iran accelerated enrichment to 60% post-JCPOA collapse (IAEA reports, 2021-2024)
Palestinian marginalization in normalization process contributed to October 7 context (Thrall, 2024)
Accords created framework for Arab-Israeli cooperation but Gaza war disrupted trajectory
Houthi Red Sea campaign explicitly framed as response to Gaza operations (Ansar Allah statements, 2023)
Hezbollah opened northern front in solidarity with Hamas after October 7 (ICG, 2024)
Gaza escalation chain led to first direct Iran-Israel military exchange April 2024
Gaza conflict imagery most shared content in MENA digital spaces in 2024 (DFRLab, 2024)
Gaza healthcare collapse and famine conditions documented by WHO and WFP (2024)
Over 1.9 million displaced within Gaza by Israeli operations (OCHA, 2024)
Gaza conflict activated multi-front regional escalation (IISS Strategic Survey, 2024)
Suez Canal traffic dropped 50% as shipping rerouted via Cape of Good Hope (IMF, 2024)
Red Sea/Suez handles 12-15% of global trade; Houthi attacks disrupted this critical chokepoint
Assad regime collapse reopened Kurdish autonomy question in northeast Syria (ICG, 2025)
Regime collapse risks ISIS detainee releases from SDF-run camps (CENTCOM, 2025)
Multiple armed factions compete for control of post-Assad Syria (Carnegie, 2025)
Russia lost its primary Middle Eastern military foothold with Assad's fall
Iran's land corridor to Hezbollah severed by regime collapse (IISS, 2025)
Nasrallah assassination and infrastructure destruction severely degraded Hezbollah capability
Hezbollah degradation weakened Iran's most capable proxy and deterrence asset
Direct strikes broke taboo on state-to-state military confrontation (Sadjadpour, 2024)
Soleimani death led Iran to decentralize proxy command, increasing autonomous action (Knights, 2020)
Assassination strengthened IRGC hardliners within Iranian decision-making
Turkish operations reduced SDF-controlled territory and displaced Kurdish populations
Turkish operations diverted SDF resources from ISIS detention and counterterrorism
Economic collapse strengthened Hezbollah's relative position as alternative service provider
Financial collapse gutted Lebanese state institutions including the military (ICG, 2022)
Diplomatic restoration reduced but did not eliminate structural rivalry (MEI, 2024)
Successful mediation enhanced China's diplomatic standing in the region (Fulton, 2023)
Social media accelerates sectarian mobilization cycles from days to hours (Zeitzoff, 2017)
Real-time conflict imagery shapes public pressure on governments to escalate or intervene
Proxy warfare requires sustained weapons supply chains (SIPRI Arms Transfers Database)
Drone/missile proliferation is a subset of broader arms proliferation dynamics
Iranian arms production sustains proxy network capabilities (UN Panel of Experts, 2023)
Proxy warfare deepens sectarian polarization and mobilization (Byman, 2019)
Proxy dynamics create escalation ladders between patron states (Mumford, 2013)
Sanctions evasion sustains conflict economies by maintaining revenue flows (Nephew, 2018)
Evasion networks allow Iran to maintain proxy funding despite sanctions (CRS, 2024)
Displacement compounds humanitarian needs and strains host country resources (UNHCR, 2023)
Secondary displacement cascades as host communities reach capacity
Climate change projected to reduce MENA precipitation 20% by 2050 (IPCC AR6)
Climate-driven agricultural collapse drives rural-to-urban and cross-border migration (Abel et al., 2019)
Climate impacts on agriculture worsen rural youth unemployment (FAO MENA, 2022)
State-backed media operations feed into social media radicalization pipelines
Competing narratives harden group identities and resistance to compromise
Iranian drone/missile technology enabled Houthi Red Sea campaign (CENTCOM, 2024)
Iranian missile and drone arsenal enabled April 2024 direct strike on Israel
Low-cost drones can threaten critical infrastructure and shipping at asymmetric cost ratios
War economies supplant and undermine formal governance structures (SIPRI, 2021)
Economic interests in instability create spoiler dynamics in peace processes
Great power arms exports to the region driven by strategic competition (SIPRI, 2024)
US-Iran and US-Saudi relationships modulate Iran-Saudi rivalry dynamics
Famine and healthcare collapse drive displacement (WFP/UNHCR, 2024)
IPC Phase 5 famine conditions confirmed in northern Gaza (IPC, 2024)
Humanitarian crises disrupt education and employment for young populations
Iran is the primary patron of the axis of resistance proxy network
Iran is the leading regional producer and proliferator of drone and missile technology
US provides $3.8 billion annual military aid and diplomatic cover to Israel (MOU, 2016)
US unilaterally withdrew from JCPOA in May 2018
US ordered the Soleimani drone strike in January 2020
Saudi Arabia is OPEC's swing producer and largest exporter
Turkey initiated and executes military operations in Syria and Iraq
Turkish military operations directly target SDF/YPG positions
HTS-led offensive from Idlib precipitated Assad regime collapse in December 2024
Energy security interests drive great power engagement in the region
Freshwater scarcity is the physical basis of hydro-political competition
Red Sea trade route vulnerability is both target and consequence of Houthi campaign
Displacement outcomes feed back into displacement cascade dynamics
State fragmentation further weakens already fragile institutions, creating reinforcing cycle
